description: Holiday fiction requires the holiday itself to be reason for gathering, source of deadline, mirror of wound, and medium of healing — not merely backdrop or decoration.

character_constraints.description: A character with an emotional wound made undeniable by the holiday — the season forces confrontation.
The person or relationship that has been broken — estrangement is the wound the holiday exposes.
Secondary characters who embody holiday pressure — neighbors, family, coworkers performing expected cheer.

relationship_constraints.description: A broken bond that the holiday makes impossible to ignore.
Social or familial obligation that creates a gathering which cannot be avoided.

place_constraints.description: The table, living room, or venue no one can exit gracefully — the holiday forces proximity.
A load-bearing, not decorative, holiday setting — the tree lot, the church, the town square during the festival.

object_constraints.description: A broken ornament, empty chair, wrong gift, or missing decoration that carries thematic meaning — not mere set dressing.
An object representing what has been lost or broken — the recipe not followed, the stocking not hung.

element_rules.description: The holiday must serve four functions simultaneously: reason for gathering, source of deadline, mirror of wound, and medium of healing.
element_rules.testable_condition: The holiday drives at least one gathering scene, creates a time constraint, reflects the protagonist's wound, and provides the medium for resolution.

element_rules.description: A broken-tradition scene and a restoration scene are both mandatory.
element_rules.testable_condition: The timeline contains a scene where a holiday tradition fails or is disrupted and a later scene where it is restored or reinvented.
